methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo that has raised $26M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method uses the company's $26M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression in biotech, yielding $65M. Peer-median anchoring was not viable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (fewer than 3 qualifying datapoints). Public-comparable anchoring could not be applied because Amber Bio's revenue is not on file, preventing meaningful P/S multiples (sector median 6.9x across 3 tickers) from being applied.

The single applicable method supports a median valuation of $65M, with a triangulated range of $55M to $75M reflecting typical estimation variance around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which precludes revenue-multiple validation, and limited peer benchmarking granularity within seed-stage biotech. Any refinement would require either disclosed financial metrics or access to a denser private-market dataset for same-stage companies.
